title: Daily Market Digest: Geopolitical Tensions Drive Weekly Losses
subtitle: Market Recap for Friday, March 27, 2026, and the Week Ending March 28, 2026

items.headline: U.S. Stocks End Week Lower Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty and Surging Oil Prices
items.summary: U.S. major indices concluded their fifth consecutive week of losses, with the S&P 500 posting its worst performance since the start of the Iran war. Escalating tensions in the Middle East, rising crude oil prices, and climbing Treasury yields fueled a risk-off sentiment, pushing investors toward defensive sectors. Recession odds have reached their highest level in years following weak labor market data and revised GDP figures.

items.statContext: Down 2.1% Friday, 3.2% Weekly, 9.9% YTD. Entered correction territory.

items.headline: Fidelity National Financial Leads Financial Gainers
items.summary: Fidelity National Financial added 5.59% for the week.

items.headline: HSBC Holdings Rises Amid De-escalation Hopes
items.summary: HSBC Holdings gained 2.91% week-over-week, reportedly benefiting from President Trump's remarks about U.S.-Iran discussions.

items.headline: Defense Stocks Surge on Geopolitical Risks
items.summary: Defense contractors like RTX saw a month-to-date increase of 22.1% as global uncertainties heightened.

items.headline: Crypto Stocks Plunge, Circle Internet Group Leads Declines
items.summary: Crypto-related stocks experienced significant losses, with Circle Internet Group falling 25.68% week-over-week.

items.headline: Coinbase Global Among Top Weekly Losers
items.summary: Coinbase Global shed 18.41% over the week as crypto markets faced a downturn.

items.headline: Meta Platforms Drops After Legal Setback
items.summary: Meta Platforms saw an 11.5% decline after losing a landmark lawsuit, impacting the Communication Services sector.

items.headline: Risk-Off Sentiment Fuels Rotation to Defensive Sectors
items.summary: Investors are actively rotating out of large-cap growth and AI hyperscalers, seeking safety in defensive sectors such as Energy, Materials, Utilities, and Consumer Staples. Technology and Consumer Discretionary were notable laggards for the week.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Conflict Drives Oil Above $100, Intensifies Inflation Fears
items.summary: The ongoing U.S.-Iran conflict and stalled diplomatic efforts pushed crude oil prices above $100 per barrel, contributing to fears of global inflation. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz continues to fuel global stagflation concerns.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady, Eyes Economic Data
items.summary: The Federal Reserve maintained interest rates in the range of 3.5% to 3.75% at its latest meeting, with markets anticipating rates to remain stable for the year. Upcoming economic data, including Fed Chair Jerome Powell's speech and the March jobs report, will be closely watched for monetary policy cues.

items.headline: Weak Labor Market and GDP Revisions Elevate Recession Odds
items.summary: Recent economic data, including a loss of 92,000 jobs in the U.S. and a downward revision of GDP from 1.4% to 0.7%, have pushed recession odds to their highest level in years. Consumer sentiment also fell to its lowest point since December 2025.
